title: Love Is A Many Splintered Thing

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening moment of the episode
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy but zero originality at this point in the series

jokes.text: The Szyslaks. Already cancelled.
jokes.setup: TV show title card for 'The Szyslaks'
jokes.punchline: Immediate cancellation notice
jokes.explanation: Quick meta joke about TV show failure - good economy and structure

jokes.text: Everything I know about women can be summed up by a jump-rope rhyme: 'Girls go to Mars to buy candy bars, boys go to Jupiter to get more stupider.'
jokes.setup: Bart explaining his knowledge about women
jokes.punchline: Childhood rhyme with bad grammar
jokes.explanation: Good character-appropriate humor with nice ironic grammar fail in the rhyme itself

jokes.text: Except I didn't have to go to Jupiter to get stupider. I had a bigger ball of gas I could study.
jokes.setup: Reference to Jupiter from the rhyme
jokes.punchline: Bart calling Homer a ball of gas
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback structure and clever insult that works on multiple levels - gas giant/flatulent father

jokes.text: Ladies, please! You can boss me around, just one at a time!
jokes.setup: Multiple female voices giving Homer driving directions
jokes.punchline: Homer's futile attempt to organize the chaos
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-awareness moment - he knows he's being bossed around but wants to make it orderly

jokes.text: Switching to male voice... so you will obey. Finally! A supervisor!
jokes.setup: GPS switches to male voice to get Homer's attention
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately treats the male voice as authority
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on gender dynamics and authority - Homer's immediate deference to male voice is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Can't I just stick it in Skinner's sloppy joe? It would actually be the only meat in said Joe.
jokes.setup: Bart being dared to put caterpillar down girl's dress
jokes.punchline: Alternative target plus cafeteria food quality joke
jokes.explanation: Standard school food joke but delivered with Bart's characteristic mischief redirection

jokes.text: Not everything can be solved with Skinner pranks, Bart. Yeah, think outside the Skinner box.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Bart's obsession with pranking Skinner
jokes.punchline: Think outside the box/Skinner box pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on the familiar phrase while being character-specific

jokes.text: I only got work as a hand model. That's your hand? Just the thumb.
jokes.setup: Mary explaining her Hollywood career as hand model
jokes.punchline: Reveals she only models one thumb
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke - hand modeling is already specific, but 'just the thumb' is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Get out of my face, cootie breath! I did miss you. You stink! Come over Saturday. I'm gonna have to push you in the mud now. Don't you pig-waller me, mister! Do what you must, darlin'.
jokes.setup: Bart and Mary's conflicted romantic interaction
jokes.punchline: Simultaneous insults and affection, ending in acceptance
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing young love through contradictory behavior - Mary's dialect adds authenticity

jokes.text: That sure makes up for my dad beating me last night. And for my dyslexia.
jokes.setup: Bart enjoying the moment with Mary
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of abuse and learning disability
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that feels more cheap shock than earned - the dyslexia tag feels tacked on

jokes.text: Thank God I wore the underwear with the hole on the side.
jokes.setup: Mary arriving early at Bart's
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of hole in underwear
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate poverty humor but fairly predictable

jokes.text: I'm a farm girl. I already milked the cows, overcame a bout of prairie madness, and made you some sausage and biscuits.
jokes.setup: Mary explaining her early morning
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of prairie madness among farm chores
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - prairie madness slipped in between mundane farm tasks is beautifully absurd

jokes.text: So like a schmuck, I treat her like day-old matzo.
jokes.setup: Bart reflecting on having the perfect girl
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission using Jewish food metaphor
jokes.explanation: Nice self-awareness and the matzo metaphor is specific and unexpected for Bart

jokes.text: Doctors Without Borders, you're now doctors without faces!
jokes.setup: Bart playing violent video game
jokes.punchline: Pun on humanitarian organization name
jokes.explanation: Clever dark wordplay that works both as video game trash talk and commentary on real organization

jokes.text: Ask his girlfriend, the game.
jokes.setup: Someone asking to steal Bart for a minute
jokes.punchline: Mary identifying video game as Bart's real girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation delivered with perfect economy - captures the relationship dynamic perfectly

jokes.text: She knows she's the apple of the corner of my eye.
jokes.setup: Bart defending his treatment of Mary
jokes.punchline: Mangled version of 'apple of my eye'
jokes.explanation: Classic malapropism that shows Bart's immaturity while being quotably wrong

jokes.text: We hope it's a nap.
jokes.setup: Explanation that the mule is taking a nap
jokes.punchline: Deadpan suggestion the mule might be dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery and economy - the uncertainty about whether their mule is alive is beautifully understated

jokes.text: That'll teach him to try to preserve our folk singing for posterity.
jokes.setup: Context of the 'city fella' situation
jokes.punchline: Treating cultural preservation as an offense
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific irony - hillbilly family being hostile to someone trying to document their culture

jokes.text: It'll prove I'm not wasting my life. You realize you just ruined the recording.
jokes.setup: Folk singer excited about NPR playing his recording
jokes.punchline: Immediately told he ruined it by talking over it
jokes.explanation: Nice timing joke - the irony of ruining what he's excited about is well-executed

jokes.text: But no one blows on a blade of grass like you.
jokes.setup: Complimenting musical talent in the house
jokes.punchline: Specific praise for grass blade music ability
jokes.explanation: Folksy compliment that's both sincere and absurd in its specificity

jokes.text: Eight more payments and I own this.
jokes.setup: Mary presenting something (presumably very humble)
jokes.punchline: Still making payments on something cheap
jokes.explanation: Good poverty humor - making payments on something that's clearly not worth it

jokes.text: But there ain't not never no place none better than home. Count the negatives, it all works out.
jokes.setup: Mary singing with terrible grammar
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about the double negatives
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware grammar joke - Mary's defense of her quadruple negative is mathematically sound and character-perfect

jokes.text: Like the Hulk in movies other than The Hulk.
jokes.setup: Bart telling Mary she has star quality
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment referencing bad Hulk movie
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference with specific knowledge of bad movies

jokes.text: We's gonna practice kissing with this French-Canadian mountain man. He's still wearing leg shackles.
jokes.setup: Sisters wanting to use the boyfriend room
jokes.punchline: Their practice partner is a shackled prisoner
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd escalation - kissing practice partner being a literal prisoner is darkly hilarious

jokes.text: What? It's the plural of Milhouse. Look it up.
jokes.setup: Bart using the word 'Milhice'
jokes.punchline: Defending his made-up plural with confidence
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with Bart's characteristic false confidence - 'Milhice' is perfectly crafted nonsense

jokes.text: More like balder and wider.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to be older and wiser
jokes.punchline: Bart's cutting physical description
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart comeback with good rhythm and economy - predictable but well-executed

jokes.text: I'll teach you to say something that sounds similar!
jokes.setup: Homer angry at Bart's insult
jokes.punchline: Threatening to teach him to rhyme
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd threat that makes no sense - Homer's brain completely misfiring is perfect

jokes.text: Isn't it funny how all people are of equal worth?
jokes.setup: Introduction of no-longer racist comedy
jokes.punchline: Forced politically correct observation
jokes.explanation: Decent satire of forced political correctness in comedy

jokes.text: Please do not boycott my line of power tools.
jokes.setup: Barry's reformed comedy act
jokes.punchline: Desperate plea about side business
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - comedian worried about his day job

jokes.text: No, they ain't. We're back together. Then you got no act.
jokes.setup: Barry claiming his jokes aren't about ex-wife anymore
jokes.punchline: Reveal that reconciliation ruins his material
jokes.explanation: Good comedy industry joke about comedians needing misery for material

jokes.text: I can finally get my suspender buckled!
jokes.setup: Devon winning the contest
jokes.punchline: His celebration is about fixing his clothes
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor about hillbilly priorities

jokes.text: Looks like we got a feud with the Peacocks now.
jokes.setup: Devon Peacock winning over Mary
jokes.punchline: Immediate assumption of family feud
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about hillbilly feuding mentality

jokes.text: Slash her face now, Bart. Slash it while she least expects it.
jokes.setup: Bart asking his heroes for advice about Mary
jokes.punchline: Video game giving violent relationship advice
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about video game violence being inappropriate for real situations

jokes.text: Um... lifting people's spirits is an interesting topic. For centuries, Neanderthal and astronaut alike have enjoyed lifting spirits.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to lift Mary's spirits
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical historical comparison
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - Bart's brain completely failing under pressure with ridiculous historical scope

jokes.text: You're givin' me book report talk.
jokes.setup: Bart's awkward attempt at being profound
jokes.punchline: Mary's perfect dismissal
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Mary's description is perfectly accurate and dismissive in four words

jokes.text: Know him? Before he was famous, he kidnapped my mother!
jokes.setup: Mary asking if he knows Billy-Bobaloo Skeeter-Rodriguez
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of kidnapping
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from name recognition to personal crime - the casual delivery sells it

jokes.text: I am the first boy to get my own liquor ad.
jokes.setup: Introduction of the handsome, girl-crazy son
jokes.punchline: His claim to fame is alcohol advertising
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about inappropriate child advertising

jokes.text: Usually, I only have music one hour a week, and the teacher spends it reading real estate listings for Fire Island.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why three hours of music was exhausting
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about teacher's distraction
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail about bad teaching - Fire Island reference adds specificity

jokes.text: At that moment, I had a vision of every time in my life an angry woman would say 'Everything's fine.'
jokes.setup: Mary saying everything's fine when it's not
jokes.punchline: Bart's prophetic realization about women
jokes.explanation: Relatable relationship humor with good narrative structure

jokes.text: I brung you regrets from my sister. She has been de-avoidably untained.
jokes.setup: Melvis delivering message about Mary
jokes.punchline: Completely mangled English
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism that's character-appropriate - 'de-avoidably untained' is wonderful nonsense

jokes.text: Woman are life's great mystery, along with why do they give you salt and pepper at a Chinese restaurant?
jokes.setup: Moe philosophizing about women
jokes.punchline: Comparison to condiment mystery
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe observation - mixing profound and mundane in character-appropriate way

jokes.text: I know a date that will turn this mighty Aphrodite into your purple rose of Cairo. Those are movies I made. It's my oeuvre. It sounds dirty, but it's not.
jokes.setup: Moe offering romantic advice with movie references
jokes.punchline: Defensive clarification about 'oeuvre'
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Moe's pretension and immediate defensive clarification is perfect

jokes.text: It's like my second wedding night, except more walkouts and there were no refunds.
jokes.setup: Moe describing the empty bar
jokes.punchline: Comparison to failed wedding night
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark Moe humor - the refunds detail makes it wonderfully specific and sad

jokes.text: Back pats? This is a breakup.
jokes.setup: Bart giving Mary back pats during their hug
jokes.punchline: Mary's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character awareness - Mary recognizing Bart's emotional distance immediately

jokes.text: There's a pot for every possum.
jokes.setup: Mary comforting Bart about their breakup
jokes.punchline: Mangled version of 'lid for every pot'
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate folk wisdom corruption - possum fits the hillbilly context

jokes.text: Hey, you're a pretty little thing, you are, but ticktock, ticktock.
jokes.setup: Joe Clabby appearing as referenced
jokes.punchline: Confirming Mary's time pressure theory
jokes.explanation: Good callback that confirms the dark joke with appropriate creepiness

jokes.text: I told you this would happen if you dated outside the family.
jokes.setup: Mary's breakup with Bart
jokes.punchline: Implication of incestuous family practices
jokes.explanation: Dark hillbilly stereotype humor - effective but relies on obvious trope

jokes.text: Sure do like forcing people to leave areas.
jokes.setup: Officer moving people along
jokes.punchline: Honest admission about enjoying the power
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - police officer's honest enjoyment of authority is darkly funny

jokes.text: You should know that me saying I don't care means I couldn't care more.
jokes.setup: Marge and Homer's relationship miscommunication
jokes.punchline: Explanation of female communication code
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor that many viewers will recognize - Marge explaining the code

jokes.text: I am sick and tired of trying to decode you like you're some kind of human being separate from myself.
jokes.setup: Marge continuing her explanation to Homer
jokes.punchline: Accusation that he doesn't see her as separate person
jokes.explanation: Excellent relationship insight with sharp writing - cuts to the heart of Homer's problem

jokes.text: You're both in the doghouse, which is misleading 'cause I still like the dog.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining that Homer and Bart are in trouble
jokes.punchline: Clarification about her feelings toward Santa's Little Helper
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge line - clever clarification of the idiom with characteristic warmth toward the dog

jokes.text: And by the way, the paint's a little faded on my parking spot.
jokes.setup: Homer acting like the hotel situation is routine
jokes.punchline: Complaint about his 'reserved' parking spot
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's entitled attitude even when banished

jokes.text: It just says somebody died in this room in the last 72 hours. Aw, man, we just missed it.
jokes.setup: Notice about death in the hotel room
jokes.punchline: Bart's disappointment at missing the death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good Bart characterization - his morbid curiosity is perfect

jokes.text: I thought I was calling the Suicide Not Line, because I'm doing great.
jokes.setup: Bart calling Mary by mistake
jokes.punchline: Defensive explanation about why he'd call suicide hotline
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's pride even when calling for help

jokes.text: Great. Turns out Adele was right. Ain't nothing better for songwriting than when your relationship takes a bolt to the brain.
jokes.setup: Mary handling the breakup well
jokes.punchline: Reference to Adele's heartbreak-fueled success
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference with character-appropriate dialect twist

jokes.text: And I can't even tell where his hair meets his forehead
jokes.setup: Mary's breakup song about Bart
jokes.punchline: Specific insult about Bart's appearance
jokes.explanation: Specific and cruel observation that captures Mary's hurt through detailed insult

jokes.text: Why did I buy her that rhyming dictionary?
jokes.setup: Listening to Mary's detailed breakup song
jokes.punchline: Realization he enabled her lyrical revenge
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - Bart's past romantic gesture now weaponized against him is perfectly crafted

jokes.text: It just poured out of me like venom from a snake.
jokes.setup: Mary describing her songwriting process
jokes.punchline: Honest description of her venomous creativity
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor that captures both the creative flow and the poison being delivered

jokes.text: It's hurting my eyes!
jokes.setup: Homer's bright wall decoration idea
jokes.punchline: Bart's physical reaction to the brightness
jokes.explanation: Basic visual gag reaction - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: It's so great here-- no wife to get mad at you for closing your laptop whenever they walk into the room.
jokes.setup: Homer enjoying the hotel life
jokes.punchline: Specific example of avoiding spousal suspicion
jokes.explanation: Relatable modern marriage humor with good specificity about suspicious behavior

jokes.text: Hard to believe that country used to rule anything.
jokes.setup: Watching terrible British romantic comedy
jokes.punchline: Comment on Britain's imperial decline
jokes.explanation: Good historical observation with typical Homer bluntness about British decline

jokes.text: And I promise to do the things I checked off within a year.
jokes.setup: Homer presenting completed chore list
jokes.punchline: Revealing his timeline for completion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - checking things off but giving himself a year to actually do them

jokes.text: But I'm the main guy in this story. Things are supposed to work out for me.
jokes.setup: Bart's romantic rejection
jokes.punchline: Appeal to narrative convention
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment - Bart's awareness of being a protagonist and expecting protagonist treatment

jokes.text: Everyone is the main guy in their own story. My friend, welcome to my story.
jokes.setup: Response to Bart's main character complaint
jokes.punchline: Philosophical response plus story shift
jokes.explanation: Excellent philosophical response followed by literal story takeover - smart and funny

jokes.text: And that's when I learned Cupid was just a fat, naked jerk with an arrow.
jokes.setup: Bart reflecting on love lessons
jokes.punchline: Cynical description of Cupid
jokes.explanation: Good cynical Bart perspective with vivid imagery - classic disillusionment humor

jokes.text: Love-- what is it? What does it mean? How do you spell it? No one knows.
jokes.setup: Bart philosophizing about love
jokes.punchline: Including spelling among love's great mysteries
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from profound to mundane - spelling being a mystery alongside love's deeper questions

jokes.text: Remember, this game is for age 14 or under. If you are older than that, please get help.
jokes.setup: Video game as cure for heartbreak
jokes.punchline: Game's self-aware age warning
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on video game culture and age-appropriate gaming

jokes.text: The widow Mary Spuckler.
jokes.setup: Message from Mary years later
jokes.punchline: She's now identified as a widow
jokes.explanation: Dark time jump joke - efficient way to show the passage of time and Mary's quick turnaround

jokes.text: You played that like a combination of Willie Mays and Paul Cézanne.
jokes.setup: Complimenting Bart's romantic strategy
jokes.punchline: Bizarre combination of athlete and artist
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random cultural comparison - baseball and impressionist painting make no sense together

tmdb_overview: Bart's dream girl, Mary Spuckler, comes to town, but his failure to pay her enough attention is having a negative impact on their relationship. Meanwhile, Homer once again runs afoul of Marge and lands in the doghouse.
